title: 10 best herbal capsules for asthma

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower contains flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and ease bronchial constriction, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant properties: The herb's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and asthma symptoms, while also protecting the lungs from damage caused by environmental pollutants.
Bronchodilatory properties: Kansas Coneflower's constituents have been shown to relax airway smooth muscle, allowing for improved airflow and bronchial dilation, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Kansas coneflower contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making them effective in treating asthma symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Kansas coneflower are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency and quality.
Cleanse the roots by gently washing them with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned roots using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 12-14 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, aiming for a dosage of 500mg per capsule.
Fill empty capsules with the ground echinacea angustifolia powder, sealing them immediately after filling to maintain potency and freshness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with asthma because they contain a unique blend of herbs that have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. The active compounds in these capsules, such as echinacein and cichoric acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the airways and ease breathing congestion. Additionally, the natural antispasmodic effects of the herb help relax bronchial muscles, allowing for easier airflow and reduced wheezing. Regular consumption of Kansas coneflower capsules may provide relief from asthma symptoms and improve overall respiratory health.

Maidenhair tree capsules helps with asthma because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to help relax bronchial smooth muscle and reduce inflammation. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties also help to alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and coughing, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, the antioxidants present in maidenhair tree capsules can help protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals, leading to improved lung function and overall health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and airway constriction, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.

Inflammation reduction: Maidenhair Tree extract has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the inflammation in the lungs and airways that occurs during an asthma attack, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Vasodilation: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve blood flow to the lungs and airways, making it easier for oxygen to reach the body's cells and reducing bronchospasm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain saponins and flavonoids that help to relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing airway constriction and making it easier to breathe.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are ripe.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat setting at 60°c for 12 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 250mg of ginkgo biloba powder with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill into vegetarian capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak capsules helps with asthma because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways. The capsules may also help to relax the muscles surrounding the bronchial tubes, allowing for easier breathing and reducing symptoms of wheezing and shortness of breath associated with asthma. Additionally, English oak has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in English oak, has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and constrict airway spasms associated with asthma.
Vasodilation: The capsules' vasodilation properties help to relax and widen airways, improving airflow and reducing symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
Antioxidant activity: English oak's antioxidant compounds help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to asthma development and exacerbation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and bronchial constriction, making them effective in alleviating asthma symptoms.
Barks: Containing tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, these compounds work together to relax airway muscles, reducing wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality quercus robur leaves and stems and dry them thoroughly in shade for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried quercus robur material into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered quercus robur with 200mg of potato starch as filler and 10mg of silicon dioxide as anti-caking agent.
Encapsulate the mixture in vegetarian capsules at a dose of 400mg per capsule, with each serving size containing 2-3 capsules three times daily.
Store the finished herbal english oak capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months after manufacturing date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with asthma because they contain mucilages, which are natural thickening agents that soothe and protect the delicate tissues of the respiratory tract. When inhaled, these mucilages form a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and irritation, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce airway constriction and excess mucus production, thereby alleviating symptoms such as wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps soothe and calm inflamed airways, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with asthma symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbs in marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and bronchial tubes, a common issue in people with asthma.

Expectorant properties: Marshmallow root has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm respiratory issues associated with asthma.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain compounds like saponins and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for alleviating asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis leaves and flowers, ensuring freshness for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or low-temperature oven method within 24 hours of harvesting.
Create a decoction by steeping one teaspoon of dried herb in one cup boiling water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve, discarding solids and measuring the resulting liquid volume.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 400 milligrams of the strained liquid extract per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden capsules helps with asthma because they contain compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the airways, reducing swelling and constriction. The capsules also contain flavonoids, which have been shown to relax smooth muscle in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, littleleaf linden has been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues, including bronchitis and allergies, making it a natural ally for individuals with asthma. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal littleleaf linden capsules can help alleviate symptoms and improve lung function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Littleleaf linden capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating symptoms of asthma.

Antispasmodic properties: The herb has antispasmodic properties that can help relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing constriction and spasms in the airways, which can make breathing more difficult for people with asthma.

Antioxidant activity: Littleleaf linden capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of chronic respiratory diseases like asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, the roots help to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating asthma symptoms.
Barks: Containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, the barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate bronchial constriction and relieve asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia cordata leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is flowering.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, dark place for at least two weeks or until crumbly.
Grind 20 grams of dried tilia cordata leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 grams of gelatin capsules in a bowl, adding 2 tablespoons of water as needed to help the mixture stick together.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, sealing each capsule immediately after filling to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with asthma because they contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin. When taken regularly, it helps to thin mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing inflammation caused by bronchial constriction. Additionally, salicin also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and relax the airways, allowing for easier breathing and improved lung function. As a natural alternative to pharmaceuticals, white willow capsules offer a gentle yet effective way to manage asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that helps to reduce inflammation and relax airway constriction in the lungs, making it easier to breathe for people with asthma.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in white willow bark capsules help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the development of asthma symptoms such as inflammation and bronchospasms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: White willow bark contains a range of anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with asthma to manage their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are rich in salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion associated with asthma.
Roots: The roots contain high amounts of flavonoids, antioxidants that help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the severity of asthma symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salix alba leaves and flowers from organic farms or wild places.
Dry the plant material using air circulation at room temperature for 1 week.
Grind dried salix alba into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered salix alba with gelatin and water to create a filling solution and fill capsules.
Seal filled capsules in dark glass bottles and store in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with asthma because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce airway constriction and swelling. By reducing inflammation in the lungs, frankincense capsules can alleviate symptoms of asthma such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the capsules' ability to relax bronchial smooth muscle helps to open up airways, making it easier for oxygen to flow and improving overall lung function. This natural approach has been shown to be effective in reducing asthma attacks and improving overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Frankincense's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate bronchial constriction and ease symptoms of asthma.

Immune-modulating property: Frankincense has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the overactive inflammatory response that is characteristic of asthma, thereby reducing the severity of attacks.

Bronchodilatory property: The sesquiterpenes present in frankincense have been found to dilate bronchial airways, which can help relieve wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce airway inflammation and alleviate symptoms of asthma.
Buds: Frankincense buds are rich in sesquiterpenes, a type of compound that has been shown to improve respiratory function and relieve bronchospasm associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Measure one hundred grams of dried boswellia serrata resin powder and sift through a fine-mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Add three tablespoons of gelatin to the powdered resin and mix until well combined in a slow speed blender or coffee grinder.
Heat three ounces of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat and add the gelatin-resin mixture stirring constantly for five minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for fifteen minutes before slowly pouring the mixture into a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to cool at room temperature before filling empty capsules with one teaspoon of the resulting gel-like substance and storing them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with asthma because they contain a natural ingredient that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for asthmatics to breathe. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to calm down the bronchial tubes and improve lung function, allowing individuals to manage their symptoms more effectively. Additionally, ashwagandha capsules have been found to increase oxygen levels in the body, which can help to alleviate respiratory distress and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which can exacerbate asthma symptoms, by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, thus reducing asthma attacks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ashwagandha has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, a common underlying cause of asthma, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and enzymes.

Bronchodilatory properties: Ashwagandha has been found to relax bronchial smooth muscle, improving lung function and increasing airflow, which can help alleviate symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ashwagandha contain a high concentration of withanolides, which help to relax airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making them effective for treating asthma.
Leaves: Ashwagandha leaves have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest withania somnifera roots and dry them thoroughly in shade for at least 7 days.
Grind dried roots into fine powder using coffee grinder or mortar pestle combination.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered ashwagandha root with 100 milligrams of gum arabic as filler material.
Encapsulate the mixture by pouring it into empty gelatin capsules and sealing tightly.
Store encapsulated ashwagandha capsules in airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with asthma because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways. The capsules also contain antioxidants, such as vitamin C and potassium, which can help protect the lungs from further damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, dandelion has been shown to increase glutathione levels, a powerful antioxidant that helps to detoxify the body, which is particularly beneficial for individuals with asthma. By taking dandelion capsules regularly, individuals with asthma may experience relief from symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce airway inflammation in asthma patients, easing congestion and breathing difficulties.

Bronchodilation: The flavonoids and triterpenoids present in dandelion may help relax bronchial smooth muscle, widening airways and improving lung function, thereby alleviating symptoms of asthma such as wheezing and coughing.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which are common underlying factors in asthma development and progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps to reduce inflammation and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
Buds: Dandelion buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to relax airway muscles and reduce bronchial spasms, making it an effective natural remedy for asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots dig up the entire root system of mature dandelion plants and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the dried dandelion roots spread the cleaned roots in a single layer on paper towels or wire racks for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried dandelion roots crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Mix with excipients combine 250 milligrams of ground dandelion root powder with 125 milligrams of silica powder and 25 milligrams of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Fill capsules carefully fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler or spoon to produce 100-120 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Onion capsules helps with asthma because they contain a compound called quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient is able to relax the airways, making it easier for air to pass through and reducing wheezing and coughing. Additionally, onion capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs, a common symptom of asthma. By taking onion capsules regularly, individuals with asthma may experience improved lung function and reduced symptoms overall.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal onion capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to asthma symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in onion capsules, such as quercetin and kaempferol, can help reduce airway inflammation and constriction, making it easier for people with asthma to breathe.

Mucus-thinning properties: Onion capsules contain mucolytic agents that help break down excess mucus in the lungs, which can make breathing easier and reduce wheezing and coughing associated with asthma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Onion roots are rich in quercetin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps alleviate bronchial constriction in asthmatic patients.
Buds: Onion buds contain allyl sulfides, which have natural expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and reduce congestion associated with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest allium cepa bulbs from a trusted source or store-bought dried bulb.
Clean the dried onion bulbs by gently brushing off any debris and impurities.
Chop the cleaned onions into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Fill #0 gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of dried and chopped onion powder per capsule using a capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal onion capsules in an airtight container protected from light and moisture at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for asthma are a natural alternative to traditional medication, designed to alleviate symptoms and improve lung function in individuals suffering from asthma. These capsules typically combine herbal extracts, such as butterbur, garlic, and ginger, which have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that help relax airway muscles, reducing wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. For example, peppermint oil has been shown to ease congestion and open up airways, while licorice root extract helps reduce inflammation in the lungs. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals with asthma can experience improved breathing, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall quality of life, allowing them to enjoy activities without being restricted by their condition.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat asthma with herbal capsules is by combining them with conventional therapies under a healthcare provider's guidance. Boswellia and turmeric have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce airway constriction, while ginkgo biloba improves lung function. Marshmallow root soothes bronchial passages, and licorice root reduces inflammation and irritation. Consult with a healthcare professional to determine the best herbal combination for your specific asthma needs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal capsules are chronic bronchitis, COPD, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. Herbal supplements like Butterbur (Petasites hybridus) and Marshmallow root (Althaea officinalis) help reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving symptoms of coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Other herbal remedies like Ginkgo biloba and Turmeric (Curcuma longa) also show promise in improving lung function and reducing oxidative stress.
